mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% in August 2021 reflects post-pandemic recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals robust consumer demand and forward economic momentum, triggering positive equity valuations and S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 617.7B in July 2021 reflects strong consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 4% year-over-year, the Fed is tightening into rising inflation, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 273.0 in August 2021 show accommodative stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression on direction_60d.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, which reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.6 in August 2021 reflects post-pandemic supply recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 48% of instances when INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerating simultaneously signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors, creating positive feedback loops that drive S&P 500 returns higher over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1534K and RSAFS at 617.7B in July 2021 show strong demand
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100K per month signals labor market cooling and reduced wage pressure, which allows the Fed to maintain accommodative policy and supports equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147.2M in August 2021 reflects strong but moderating job growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with moderating labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) turning negative signal monetary accommodation that reduces the cost of capital, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity risk premiums compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% with CPIAUCSL inflation ~4% creates deeply negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the real rate proxy shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 2% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown and recession risk, which triggers equity market repricing and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 21.4T in Q1 2021 reflects strong post-pandemic recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.
